segments.text: This is the burden of the word of the LORD to Israel through Malachi:[^1]
I have loved you,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
says the LORD.
But you ask,
How have You loved us?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Was not Esau Jacob’s brother?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Yet Jacob I have loved,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: but Esau I have hated,[^2] and I have made his mountains a wasteland and left his inheritance to the desert jackals.[^3]
segments.speaker: the Lord

footnotes.text: + Or to the serpents of the wilderness or to the dragons of the wilderness

segments.text: Though Edom may say,
We have been devastated, but we will rebuild the ruins,
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: this is what the LORD of Hosts says:
They may build, but I will demolish. They will be called the Land of Wickedness, and a people with whom the LORD is indignant forever.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: You will see this with your own eyes, and you yourselves will say, ‘The LORD is great—even beyond the borders of Israel.’
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: A son honors his father, and a servant his master. But if I am a father, where is My honor? And if I am a master, where is your fear of Me?
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: says the LORD of Hosts to you priests who despise My name.
But you ask, ‘How have we despised Your name?’
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text:
By presenting defiled food on My altar.
But you ask, ‘How have we defiled You[^4]?’
By saying that the table of the LORD is contemptible.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: When you offer blind animals for sacrifice, is it not wrong? And when you present the lame and sick ones, is it not wrong? Try offering them to your governor! Would he be pleased with you or show you favor?
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: asks the LORD of Hosts.
But ask now for God’s favor. Will He be gracious? Since this has come from your hands, will He show you favor?
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: asks the LORD of Hosts.
Oh, that one of you would shut the temple doors, so that you would no longer kindle useless fires on My altar! I take no pleasure in you,
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: says the LORD of Hosts,
and I will accept no offering from your hands.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: For My name will be great among the nations, from where the sun rises to where it sets. In every place, incense and pure offerings will be presented in My name, because My name will be great among the nations,
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: says the LORD of Hosts.
But you profane it when you say, ‘The table of the Lord is defiled, and as for its fruit, its food is contemptible.’
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: You also say: ‘Oh, what a nuisance!’ And you turn up your nose at it,
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: says the LORD of Hosts.
You bring offerings that are stolen, lame, or sick! Should I accept these from your hands?
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: asks the LORD.
But cursed is the deceiver who has an acceptable male in his flock and vows to give it, but sacrifices a defective animal to the Lord. For I am a great King,
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: says the LORD of Hosts, “and My name is to be feared among the nations.
